content.content:
2763	pub fn parse(
2764	    state: &mut LuaState,
2765	    dyd: DynData,
2766	    source: &[u8],
2767	    name: &[u8],
2768	    firstchar: i32,
2769	) -> Result<Box<LuaProto>, LuaError> {
2770	    let source_str = state.intern_str(name)?;
2771	    let envn_str = state.intern_str(lua_lex::LUA_ENV)?;
2772	
2773	    let rest_bytes: Vec<u8> = source.iter().skip(1).copied().collect();
2774	    let z = lua_lex::ZIO::from_bytes(rest_bytes);
2775	
2776	    let lex_ls = lua_lex::LexState {
2777	        current: firstchar,
2778	        linenumber: 1,
2779	        lastline: 1,
2780	        t: lua_lex::Token::eos(),
2781	        lookahead: lua_lex::Token::eos(),
2782	        fs: None,
2783	        z,
2784	        buff: lua_lex::LexBuffer::new(),
2785	        h: None,
2786	        dyd: None,
2787	        source: source_str.0.clone(),
2788	        envn: envn_str.0.clone(),
2789	    };
2790	
2791	    let mut lexstate = LexState {
2792	        current: lex_ls.current,
2793	        linenumber: lex_ls.linenumber,
2794	        lastline: lex_ls.lastline,
2795	        t: LexToken::default(),
2796	        lookahead: LexToken::default(),
2797	        fs: None,
2798	        dyd,
2799	        source: Some(source_str.clone()),
2800	        envn: Some(GcRef(lex_ls.envn.clone())),
2801	        lex: lex_ls,
2802	    };
2803	    // C: luaX_setinput is the only setup the C parser performs before
2804	    //   `mainfunc`; it does NOT pre-read the first token. `mainfunc` itself
2805	    //   issues the initial `luaX_next` once its prelude (open_func, vararg
2806	    //   marker, _ENV upvalue) is in place.
2807	
2808	    let mut main_proto = Box::new(LuaProto::placeholder());
2809	    main_proto.source = Some(source_str);
2810	    main_proto.is_vararg = true;
2811	    let main_fs = FuncState {
2812	        f: main_proto,
2813	        prev: None,
2814	        bl: None,
2815	        pc: 0,
2816	        lasttarget: 0,
2817	        previousline: 0,
2818	        nk: 0,
2819	        np: 0,
2820	        nabslineinfo: 0,
2821	        firstlocal: 0,
2822	        firstlabel: 0,
2823	        ndebugvars: 0,
2824	        nactvar: 0,
2825	        nups: 0,
2826	        freereg: 0,
2827	        iwthabs: 0,
2828	        needclose: false,
2829	    };
2830	
2831	    mainfunc(&mut lexstate, state, main_fs)
2832	}
2833	
2834	/// Convert a `lua_lex::TokenValue` into the local `parse::TokenValue` flat shape.
2835	///
2836	/// The parser's local `LexState` predates the lex-side enum and uses a flat
2837	/// (r, i, ts) record; this picks out whichever variant the lexer produced.
2838	fn local_token_value(v: &lua_lex::TokenValue) -> TokenValue {
2839	    match v {
2840	        lua_lex::TokenValue::None => TokenValue::default(),
2841	        lua_lex::TokenValue::Float(r) => TokenValue { r: *r, i: 0, ts: None },
2842	        lua_lex::TokenValue::Int(i) => TokenValue { r: 0.0, i: *i, ts: None },
2843	        lua_lex::TokenValue::Str(s) => TokenValue { r: 0.0, i: 0, ts: Some(GcRef(s.clone())) },
2844	    }
2845	}
2846	
2847	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2848	// PORT STATUS
2849	//   source:        src/lparser.c  (1968 lines, 95 functions)
2850	//   target_crate:  lua-parse
2851	//   confidence:    medium
2852	//   todos:         184
2853	//   port_notes:    14
2854	//   unsafe_blocks: 0
2855	//   notes:         All 95 functions translated with correct logical structure.
2856	//                  184 TODO(port) stubs for cross-crate calls (lua_code::*,
2857	//                  lua_lex::*, lua_vm state allocation). Key design choices:
2858	//                  BlockCnt/LhsAssign use Option<Box<...>> chains; FuncState
2859	//                  uses Box<LuaProto> (not GcRef) for mutable access during
2860	//                  build. singlevaraux FuncState.prev chain traversal (upvalue
2861	//                  capture across closures) is a known TODO — needs recursive
2862	//                  descent through fs.prev without double-mutable-borrow.

content.content:
1	//! `LuaProto` — compiled function prototype. Mirrors C-Lua's `Proto` struct
2	//! but uses Rust idioms (Vec instead of pointer+size pairs).
3	
4	use crate::gc::GcRef;
5	use crate::opcode::Instruction;
6	use crate::string::LuaString;
7	use crate::value::LuaValue;
8	
9	#[derive(Debug)]
10	pub struct LuaProto {
11	    pub numparams: u8,
12	    pub is_vararg: bool,
13	    pub maxstacksize: u8,
14	    pub upvalues: Vec<UpvalDesc>,
15	    pub k: Vec<LuaValue>,
16	    pub code: Vec<Instruction>,
17	    pub p: Vec<GcRef<LuaProto>>,
18	    pub lineinfo: Vec<i8>,
19	    pub abslineinfo: Vec<AbsLineInfo>,
20	    pub locvars: Vec<LocalVar>,
21	    pub linedefined: i32,
22	    pub lastlinedefined: i32,
23	    pub source: Option<GcRef<LuaString>>,
24	}
25	
26	impl LuaProto {
27	    pub fn placeholder() -> Self {
28	        LuaProto {
29	            numparams: 0,
30	            is_vararg: false,
31	            maxstacksize: 2,
32	            upvalues: Vec::new(),
33	            k: Vec::new(),
34	            code: Vec::new(),
35	            p: Vec::new(),
36	            lineinfo: Vec::new(),
37	            abslineinfo: Vec::new(),
38	            locvars: Vec::new(),
39	            linedefined: 0,
40	            lastlinedefined: 0,
41	            source: None,
42	        }
43	    }
44	}
45	
46	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
47	pub struct UpvalDesc {
48	    pub name: Option<GcRef<LuaString>>,
49	    pub instack: bool,
50	    pub idx: u8,
51	    pub kind: u8,
52	}
53	
54	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
55	pub struct LocalVar {
56	    pub varname: GcRef<LuaString>,
57	    pub startpc: i32,
58	    pub endpc: i32,
59	}
60	
61	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
62	pub struct AbsLineInfo {
63	    pub pc: i32,
64	    pub line: i32,
65	}
66

1	//! Bytecode emitter and opcode definitions.
2	//!
3	//! Phase A scope. See PORT_STRATEGY.md §4.
4	
5	pub mod codegen;
6	pub mod opcode_names;
7	pub mod opcodes;
8	
9	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
10	// PORT STATUS
11	//   source:        (composite crate; see opcode_names.rs for the first port)
12	//   target_crate:  lua-code
13	//   confidence:    high
14	//   todos:         0

17	//   notes:         module aggregator
18	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1	//! Code generator for Lua 5.4.
2	//!
3	//! Ports `src/lcode.c` (1875 lines, ~60 functions) and the public interface
4	//! from `src/lcode.h`.
5	//!
6	//! Phase A: faithful logic translation — does not yet compile.
7	//!
8	//! TODO(port): FuncState.f is GcRef<LuaProto> per types.tsv but the code
9	//! generator mutates it directly.  Phase B must wrap LuaProto in
10	//! RefCell<LuaProto> (or similar) inside GcRef so that `fs.f.code`, `fs.f.k`,
11	//! etc. are mutable.  All `fs.f.<field>` accesses below assume direct mutability.
12	//!
13	//! TODO(port): LexState.L (the &mut LuaState back-pointer) was removed per
14	//! types.tsv.  Functions that call luaM_growvector / luaH_get / luaC_barrier /
15	//! luaO_rawarith carry an explicit `state: &mut LuaState` parameter here.
16	//! The caller (lua-parse) must thread it through.
17	
18	// C: /* $Id: lcode.c $ */
19	// C: #define lcode_c
20	// C: #define LUA_CORE
21	
22	use crate::opcodes::{
23	    Instruction, OpCode, OpMode,
24	    MAXARG_A, MAXARG_B, MAXARG_C, MAXARG_BX, MAXARG_AX, MAXARG_S_J,
25	    OFFSET_S_BX, OFFSET_S_J, OFFSET_S_C,
26	    NO_REG, MAXINDEXRK, LFIELDS_PER_FLUSH,
27	};
28	
29	use lua_types::error::LuaError;
30	use lua_types::value::LuaValue;
31	use lua_types::string::LuaString;
32	use lua_types::gc::GcRef;
33	use lua_types::tagmethod::TagMethod;
34	
35	// ─── PHASE B PLACEHOLDERS ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
36	//
37	// The following types live in `lua-parse` (`FuncState`, `ExprDesc`, `ExprKind`,
38	// `LexState`) and `lua-vm` (`LuaState`, `LuaTable`).  Importing them from those
39	// crates would create a dependency cycle (`lua-vm` depends on `lua-code`), so
40	// we keep stub shapes here in Phase B.  The real definitions land when those
41	// crates fully exist; the field/method signatures below are inferred from the
42	// concrete usage in `codegen.rs`.
43	
44	/// PHASE B: real definition lives in lua-types (`lua_types::proto::LuaProto`),
45	/// but it stores `Vec<lua_types::opcode::Instruction>` — a barebones u32 wrapper
46	/// without the `arg_a` / `set_opcode` / `is_in_top` / etc. methods that the
47	/// code generator calls.  Those methods live on the local
48	/// `crate::opcodes::Instruction` type.  Until the two `Instruction` types are
49	/// unified (Phase B), `FuncState` carries a local proto shape that uses the
50	/// methodful local `Instruction`.
51	pub struct LuaProto {
52	    pub numparams: u8,
53	    pub is_vararg: bool,
54	    pub maxstacksize: u8,
55	    pub k: Vec<LuaValue>,
56	    pub code: Vec<Instruction>,
57	    pub lineinfo: Vec<i8>,
58	    pub abslineinfo: Vec<AbsLineInfo>,
59	}
60	
61	/// PHASE B: mirrors `lua_types::proto::AbsLineInfo`; kept local so it pairs
62	/// with the local `LuaProto` placeholder above.
63	#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
64	pub struct AbsLineInfo {
65	    pub pc: i32,
66	    pub line: i32,
67	}
68	
69	/// PHASE B: real definition lives in lua-parse; placeholder shape inferred
70	/// from codegen usage.
71	pub struct FuncState {
72	    pub f: LuaProto,
73	    pub pc: i32,
74	    pub lasttarget: i32,
75	    pub previousline: i32,
76	    pub iwthabs: u8,
77	    pub nabslineinfo: i32,
78	    pub nactvar: u8,
79	    pub freereg: u8,
80	    pub nk: i32,
81	    pub needclose: bool,
82	}
83	
84	/// PHASE B: real definition lives in lua-parse; placeholder shape inferred
85	/// from codegen usage.  Mirrors the C `expdesc` struct (a tagged kind plus a
86	/// payload union); the union becomes a plain struct here so every variant's
87	/// fields are addressable.
88	pub struct ExprDesc {
89	    pub k: ExprKind,
90	    pub t: i32,
91	    pub f: i32,
92	    pub u: ExprDescU,
93	}
94	
95	impl Clone for ExprDesc {
96	    fn clone(&self) -> Self {
97	        ExprDesc {
98	            k: self.k,
99	            t: self.t,
100	            f: self.f,
101	            u: ExprDescU {
102	                info: self.u.info,
103	                ival: self.u.ival,
104	                nval: self.u.nval,
105	                strval: self.u.strval.clone(),
106	                var: self.u.var,
107	                ind: self.u.ind,
108	            },
109	        }
110	    }
111	}
112	
113	impl ExprDesc {
114	    /// C: `static const expdesc ef = {VKINT, {0}, NO_JUMP, NO_JUMP};`
115	    /// PHASE B: a zero `VKINT` literal used as a synthetic operand by the
116	    /// constant-folding helpers in `prefix()`.
117	    pub fn const_zero() -> Self {
118	        todo!("PHASE B: ExprDesc::const_zero — real shape requires GcRef<LuaString> default")
119	    }
120	}

content.content:
320	/// In C: stack-allocated in `body()`, chained via raw `*prev` pointer.
321	/// In Rust: heap-allocated via `Option<Box<FuncState>>` in LexState.
322	#[derive(Debug)]
323	pub struct FuncState {
324	    /// C: f — the Proto being built.
325	    /// PORT NOTE: types.tsv maps this to GcRef<LuaProto>; we use Box<LuaProto>
326	    ///   during compilation to avoid RefCell overhead. close_func hands it to
327	    ///   the GC/parent at close time.
328	    pub f: Box<LuaProto>,
329	    /// C: prev — enclosing FuncState (raw pointer in C; owned Box here).
330	    pub prev: Option<Box<FuncState>>,
331	    /// C: bl — innermost active block
332	    pub bl: Option<Box<BlockCnt>>,
333	    /// C: pc — next bytecode position to emit
334	    pub pc: i32,
335	    /// C: lasttarget — pc of last 'jump label'
336	    pub lasttarget: i32,
337	    /// C: previousline — last line saved in lineinfo
338	    pub previousline: i32,
339	    /// C: nk — number of constants emitted
340	    pub nk: i32,
341	    /// C: np — number of nested prototypes emitted
342	    pub np: i32,
343	    /// C: nabslineinfo — number of absolute line-info records
344	    pub nabslineinfo: i32,
345	    /// C: firstlocal — index of first local var in dyd.actvar
346	    pub firstlocal: i32,
347	    /// C: firstlabel — index of first label in dyd.label
348	    pub firstlabel: i32,
349	    /// C: ndebugvars — entries in f.locvars
350	    pub ndebugvars: i16,
351	    /// C: nactvar — number of active locals
352	    pub nactvar: u8,
353	    /// C: nups — number of upvalues
354	    pub nups: u8,
355	    /// C: freereg — next free register
356	    pub freereg: u8,
357	    /// C: iwthabs — instructions since last absolute line info
358	    pub iwthabs: u8,
359	    /// C: needclose — function must close upvalues on return
360	    pub needclose: bool,
361	}
362	
363	// ── ConsControl ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
364	
365	/// C: ConsControl — state for parsing a table constructor.
366	/// PORT NOTE: C stores `expdesc *t` as a pointer to the caller's expdesc.
367	///   Rust stores a copy of the table descriptor; callers must sync back
368	///   if they mutate it. Phase B may restructure.
369	#[derive(Debug)]
370	pub struct ConsControl {
371	    /// C: v — last list item read
372	    pub v: ExprDesc,
373	    /// C: *t — table descriptor (copied; see PORT NOTE above)
374	    pub t: ExprDesc,
375	    /// C: nh — total number of record elements
376	    pub nh: i32,
377	    /// C: na — number of array elements already stored
378	    pub na: i32,
379	    /// C: tostore — array elements pending store

880	    /// C: `SETARG_sJ(i, j)` — stores `j + OFFSET_S_J` in the sJ field.
881	    #[inline]
882	    pub fn set_arg_s_j(&mut self, j: i32) {
883	        self.set_arg((j + OFFSET_S_J) as u32, POS_S_J, SIZE_S_J);
884	    }
885	
886	    // ── Instruction builders ──────────────────────────────────────────────
887	
888	    /// Build an `iABC` instruction.
889	    ///
890	    /// C: `CREATE_ABCk(o, a, b, c, k)`
891	    #[inline]
892	    pub fn abck(op: OpCode, a: u32, b: u32, c: u32, k: u32) -> Self {
893	        Self(
894	            ((op as u32) << POS_OP)
895	                | (a << POS_A)
896	                | (b << POS_B)
897	                | (c << POS_C)
898	                | (k << POS_K),
899	        )
900	    }
901	
902	    /// Build an `iABx` instruction.
903	    ///
904	    /// C: `CREATE_ABx(o, a, bc)`
905	    #[inline]
906	    pub fn abx(op: OpCode, a: u32, bc: u32) -> Self {
907	        Self(((op as u32) << POS_OP) | (a << POS_A) | (bc << POS_BX))
908	    }
909	
910	    /// Build an `iAx` instruction.
911	    ///
912	    /// C: `CREATE_Ax(o, a)`
913	    #[inline]
914	    pub fn ax(op: OpCode, a: u32) -> Self {
915	        Self(((op as u32) << POS_OP) | (a << POS_AX))
916	    }
917	
918	    /// Build an `isJ` instruction.
919	    ///
920	    /// C: `CREATE_sJ(o, j, k)`
921	    #[inline]
922	    pub fn sj(op: OpCode, j: u32, k: u32) -> Self {
923	        Self(((op as u32) << POS_OP) | (j << POS_S_J) | (k << POS_K))
924	    }
925	
926	    // ── Mode query helpers (isOT / isIT) ──────────────────────────────────
927	
928	    /// True if this instruction sets `L->top` for the next instruction.
929	    ///

content.content:
715	/// C: `typedef unsigned int Instruction;` (see llimits.h)
716	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
717	#[repr(transparent)]
718	pub struct Instruction(pub u32);
719	
720	impl Instruction {
721	    // ── Low-level field accessors ─────────────────────────────────────────
722	
723	    /// Extract a bit-field of `size` bits at position `pos`.
724	    ///
725	    /// C: `getarg(i, pos, size)` — `cast_int(((i)>>(pos)) & MASK1(size,0))`
726	    #[inline]
727	    pub const fn get_arg(self, pos: u32, size: u32) -> u32 {
728	        (self.0 >> pos) & ((1u32 << size) - 1)
729	    }
730	
731	    /// Set a bit-field of `size` bits at position `pos` to `v`.
732	    ///
733	    /// C: `setarg(i, v, pos, size)`
734	    #[inline]
735	    pub fn set_arg(&mut self, v: u32, pos: u32, size: u32) {
736	        let mask = ((1u32 << size) - 1) << pos;
737	        self.0 = (self.0 & !mask) | ((v << pos) & mask);
738	    }
739	
740	    // ── Opcode field ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
741	
742	    /// Extract the opcode.
743	    ///
744	    /// C: `GET_OPCODE(i)` — `cast(OpCode, ((i)>>POS_OP) & MASK1(SIZE_OP,0))`

content.text:
The original panic is resolved. The new error is a different `todo!()` blocker that the loop will handle next.

## Report

**Root cause** — `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:1419` in `close_func`: the C-Lua `luaK_ret(fs, nvarstack(fs), 0)` call (which emits the implicit `OP_RETURN0` terminating every chunk) was left as a `TODO(port)`. Because no instruction was ever pushed into `fs.f.code`, the parser handed the VM a `LuaProto` with `code.len() == 0`. When `vm::execute` then fetched `proto.code[0]` via `LuaState::proto_code` (`crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1382`), the indexing panicked.

**Fix** — Replaced the TODO in `close_func` with a real emission: compute `first = nvarstack(ls, fs)`, build `OP_RETURN0` via `lua_code::opcodes::Instruction::abck(OpCode::Return0, first, 1, 0, 0)`, then push it into `fs.f.code` (resizing if needed), keep `lineinfo` in lockstep, and bump `fs.pc`. The subsequent `code.truncate(pc)` therefore preserves the new instruction.

**New error** —
```
thread 'main' panicked at crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs:83:9:
not yet implemented: phase-b: OpCode decode from instruction word
```
The chunk now contains real bytecode and reaches dispatch; the next blocker is `InstructionExt::opcode()` still being `todo!()`. No temporary `eprintln!()` was added.
